partyscape 0.1.0

  • Changelog

partyscape

Diversity profiles, profile crossings, and beta diversity for party-system research. Implements the three analytical tools proposed in Neunhoeffer (forthcoming) on a small, tidyverse-free footprint.

Installation 

# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("mneunhoe/partyscape")

A 20-line tour

All examples below work on party-labeled compositions — columns carry party identity, never rank. The paper’s central point is that sorting shares by size (so “column 1 = largest party that year”) discards the information alpha_beta_gamma() is designed to detect, so the package encourages you to keep party identity intact.

library(partyscape)

# Germany 2009 Bundestag — columns named by party, not sorted by size.
germany_2009 <- c(
  CDU   = 0.312, CSU   = 0.072, SPD   = 0.235,
  FDP   = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122
)

# UK 1997 Commons — a different party set, also named (not sorted).
uk_1997 <- c(
  Labour       = 0.634, Conservative = 0.250, LibDem   = 0.070,
  UUP          = 0.015, SNP          = 0.009, PlaidCymru = 0.006,
  SDLP         = 0.005, DUP          = 0.003, SinnFein = 0.003,
  Independent  = 0.005
)

# 1. Diversity profile: a steep UK curve vs. a flat German one.
diversity_profile(uk_1997)
diversity_profile(germany_2009)

# 2. Profile crossings across two systems.
crossings(uk_1997, germany_2009, q = seq(0, 5, 0.01))

# 3. Beta diversity across Germany's 2002-2005-2009 trajectory. Columns
# share the same party slots across rows so CDU/SPD swapping as the
# largest party between 2002 and 2005 is visible to the decomposition.
germany <- rbind(
  `2002` = c(CDU = 0.315, CSU = 0.096, SPD = 0.416,
             FDP = 0.078, Gruene = 0.091, Linke = 0.003),
  `2005` = c(CDU = 0.293, CSU = 0.075, SPD = 0.362,
             FDP = 0.099, Gruene = 0.083, Linke = 0.088),
  `2009` = c(CDU = 0.312, CSU = 0.072, SPD = 0.235,
             FDP = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122)
)
alpha_beta_gamma(germany, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5),
                 years = c(2002, 2005, 2009))

What the package provides

  • Pillar 1 — diversity profiles: hill_number(), diversity_profile(), evenness_profile().
  • Pillar 2 — profile crossings: crossings() (linear-interpolation or sign-change detector).
  • Pillar 3 — beta diversity: alpha_beta_gamma(), collapse_to_elections().
  • Scalar summaries: hhi(), rae(), enp(), dominance_gap(), top_two().
  • Null model: taagepera_allik_shares().
  • ParlGov accessors: fetch_parlgov(), parlgov_seat_shares().
  • Plotting: autoplot() / plot() methods for every S3 class, plus a partyscape_palette() helper.
